chapter_title: Causeways, Bridges, Tunnels, Turnpikes, Ferries, & Highways in Certain Counties
text:
(a) To acquire property useful in connection with a project, a county may enter on any real property, water, or premises to make a survey, sounding, or examination.

(b) A county may acquire by eminent domain property to use in or useful for a project under this chapter.

(c) Except as provided by Section 284.0615 (Declaration of Taking by Certain Counties) , if applicable, the county is entitled to immediate possession of property subject to a condemnation proceeding brought by the county after:

(1) a tender of a bond or other security in an amount sufficient to secure the owner for damages; and

(2) the approval of the bond or security by the court.

(d) Subject to the reimbursement requirements of Section 373.102 (Reimbursement for Use of Right-of-way) , a county has full easements and rights-of-way through, across, under, and over any property owned by this state that are necessary or convenient to construct, acquire, or efficiently operate a project under this chapter.
markdown:
# Transp. Code
## Title 6
### Chapter 284. Causeways, Bridges, Tunnels, Turnpikes, Ferries, & Highways in Certain Counties
##### § 284.061 — Acquisition of Property

(a) To acquire property useful in connection with a project, a county may enter on any real property, water, or premises to make a survey, sounding, or examination.

(b) A county may acquire by eminent domain property to use in or useful for a project under this chapter.

(c) Except as provided by Section 284.0615 (Declaration of Taking by Certain Counties) , if applicable, the county is entitled to immediate possession of property subject to a condemnation proceeding brought by the county after:

    (1) a tender of a bond or other security in an amount sufficient to secure the owner for damages; and

    (2) the approval of the bond or security by the court.

(d) Subject to the reimbursement requirements of Section 373.102 (Reimbursement for Use of Right-of-way) , a county has full easements and rights-of-way through, across, under, and over any property owned by this state that are necessary or convenient to construct, acquire, or efficiently operate a project under this chapter.

*Acts 2011, 82nd Leg., R.S., Ch. 1196 (S.B.19), Sec. 6, eff. June 17, 2011.*

a tender of a bond or other security in an amount sufficient to secure the owner for damages; and
the approval of the bond or security by the court.

subsections.label: d
subsections.text: Subject to the reimbursement requirements of Section 373.102 (Reimbursement for Use of Right-of-way) , a county has full easements and rights-of-way through, across, under, and over any property owned by this state that are necessary or convenient to construct, acquire, or efficiently operate a project under this chapter.
